name: Chaos
description: Six-channel aleatoric CV and gate generator. Master clock 10ms–10s, per-channel probability/width/swing/ratcheting, quantized CV with voltage-window and ground-transpose, 1–32 step looping, 60 slots, full chaos randomization.

controls.name: Encoder
controls.description: Rotates to select active channel (no press needed). Rotating past channel 6 selects ALL channels for simultaneous edits. Press to fire the Chaos randomization function.

controls.name: Modifier buttons (prob / width / time / ratcheting / ground / random-window)
controls.description: Five black buttons providing direct access to gate and CV parameters. Hold + rotate encoder to edit. Avoids menu diving.
controls.detail: hold = edit · LED bar shows value

controls.name: quant / slew button
controls.description: Hold + encoder selects quantization scale (24 scales across 3 banks). Tap + hold + rotate sets slew amount as percentage of step duration. Also used with encoder for root-note selection.
controls.detail: hold = scale · tap+hold = slew · scale banks cycle past end

controls.name: input assign button
controls.description: Opens per-channel assignment menu for the four top-row inputs (clock, loop, CV, chaos). Flip each input's link on/off per channel. Also toggles channel run/stop (double-tap then press modifiers).

controls.name: loop button (white)
controls.description: Enters loop mode capturing the last 1–32 steps. Hold + encoder to choose loop length. Hold + modifier-button to transpose/alter the loop non-destructively. Hold + memory-access to reset loop to original state.
controls.detail: 1–32 steps · 4 pages of 8 LEDs · value LED 9/10 shows active page

controls.name: save/recall / memory access button
controls.description: Holds to enter 60-slot memory menu (6 banks × 10 slots). Press encoder to save; press probability button to clear. Double-tap to reset channel to defaults. Starts up recalling last-used slot.
controls.detail: 60 slots · auto-recall at boot · double-tap = channel reset

inputs.name: clock in
inputs.description: External gate/trigger input. 50% duty 8V typical. When patched, Channel 1 can sync to external clock; also acts as global start/stop (pulse interrupts = stop).

inputs.name: loop in
inputs.description: Gate input that triggers loop mode for assigned channels. Per-channel routing via input assign menu.

inputs.name: cv in
inputs.description: 0–8V CV input. Routed per channel for transpose (ground-transpose via CV), slot recall (1V/slot increments), or Sample-and-Hold replacement of the internal random generator.

inputs.name: chaos in
inputs.description: Gate input that fires the Chaos randomization function for assigned channels. Lets sequence-driven chaos injections be patched without pressing the encoder.

outputs.name: clock out
outputs.description: Master clock gate output. Always active regardless of channel state.

outputs.name: Gate 1–6
outputs.description: Per-channel gate outputs. 8V when high. Each has its own probability/width/time/ratcheting/swing settings.

outputs.name: CV 1–6
outputs.description: Per-channel CV outputs. 0–8V range in 1V steps (LED bar reflects value between steps). Range limited by Voltage Window and Ground Transpose modifiers.

behaviors.name: Per-channel clock sync or free-run
behaviors.trigger: input assign menu · clock LED color per channel
behaviors.description: Each channel independently syncs to master clock (green LED) or runs on its own millisecond-timebase (white LED). Unsynced Time ranges 10ms to 10s. Synced channels get 1/8 to 8/1 multiplication/division range, extendable to /16 and /32. Allows polyrhythms and cross-rate drift per channel.

behaviors.name: Probability as coin-toss per gate
behaviors.description: Each scheduled gate has a 0–100% chance of firing. Setting to 0 mutes the channel. When Ratcheting is set, probability applies per ratchet sub-step so ratchet patterns naturally thin out probabilistically.

behaviors.name: Ratcheting sub-divisions
behaviors.description: Divides a single step into 1:1 through 8:1 sub-gates. Each sub-gate keeps the step's probability and width, producing rolling drum fills. Combined with probability it produces dynamic rhythmic fluctuations while holding the same CV output (same note).

behaviors.name: Swing delay per channel
behaviors.description: Shifts synced-channel timing by 0, 1/4, 2/4, or 3/4 of a master-clock step. Per-channel so different channels can swing differently; classic upbeat grooves without a dedicated swing module.

behaviors.name: Voltage window limits CV range
behaviors.description: Caps the upper limit of the random voltage from 1 to 8V. Essential when the CV controls a VCO to stay within one octave. Full range = LED flashes orange. Partial windows shown as white LED count.

behaviors.name: Ground transpose raises floor
behaviors.description: Shifts the lower limit of the voltage window up, transposing the whole random range without widening it. Unquantized: 1V/12 per encoder dent (semitone). Quantized: moves to next scale note. Combined with Voltage Window, gives a bounded pitch range per channel.

behaviors.name: Quantization to 24 scales
behaviors.description: Three scale banks × 8 scales each (first bank includes unquantized, chromatic, octave, pentatonic, blues, Arabic, pelog, hirajōshi, Chinese; second bank = 7 modes of major; third bank = 7 modes of melodic minor). Root note set independently via quant/slew + press + hold.

behaviors.name: CV-IN Sample and Hold
behaviors.trigger: input assign + encoder to 3rd LED · press encoder
behaviors.description: Replaces the internal random generator on the selected channel with the external CV input. Quantization, voltage windows, and transpose still apply — so an LFO or envelope feeds the channel's CV output through the full scale engine. Slew doesn't work in this mode (external CV can't be predicted).

behaviors.name: Loop capture 1–32 steps
behaviors.description: Freezes the last N steps into a repeating loop. Value bar splits into 4 pages of 8 LEDs with 9th/10th LEDs indicating which page you're editing. Loop modifiers (loop-prob, loop-width, etc.) operate RELATIVE to original values — loop probability maxes at channel's original probability, loop width scales original width up/down.

behaviors.name: Loop direction and reset
behaviors.trigger: hold loop button + input-assign or memory-access
behaviors.description: Hold loop then press input-assign to invert direction (backwards). Hold loop then press quant/slew to reset the loop counter to step 1. Double-tap memory-access to restore loop to its captured state.

behaviors.name: Chaos function re-rolls modifiers
behaviors.trigger: press encoder · or gate into chaos in
behaviors.description: Randomizes every modifier value for selected channel(s). Entropy per modifier (set via hold modifier + encoder) limits how far new values drift from current — 0% = no change, 100% = totally new. Lets the user zap between mood variants (high entropy) or add subtle jitter (low entropy).

behaviors.name: 60 slot memory with CV recall
behaviors.trigger: CV in routed to slot recall · 0–8V scans 10 slots
behaviors.description: 6 banks of 10 slots each (60 total). 0–8V on CV in advances in 1V increments across a selected bank's ten slots. This is GLOBAL: overrides per-channel CV assignments. At startup Chaos auto-recalls the last saved/used slot.

behaviors.name: All-channel edit
behaviors.trigger: encoder rotated past channel 6 → all LEDs red
behaviors.description: Selects all six channels simultaneously. Any modifier adjustment or chaos press is applied to every channel at once. Useful for global entropy zaps and reset-to-defaults operations.

patchIdeas.name: 6-voice chaotic drum kit
patchIdeas.patch: All channels synced to master · each channel's Time set to 1:1, 2:1, 1:2, 3:1, 4:1, 1:4 · probability ~70% per channel · six drums triggered by gates 1-6 · polyrhythmic chaos drums
patchIdeas.diagram:
Chaos:
* CH 1-6: synced
* CH 1: time 1:1, prob 80%
* CH 2: time 2:1, prob 60%
* CH 3: time 1:2, prob 70%
* CH 4-6: various ratios

Clock Source:

- Clock Source (Out) c> Chaos (clock in)
- Chaos (Gate 1) t> Drum 1 (Trig)
- Chaos (Gate 2) t> Drum 2 (Trig)
- Chaos (Gate 3) t> Drum 3 (Trig)
- Chaos (Gate 4) t> Drum 4 (Trig)
- Chaos (Gate 5) t> Drum 5 (Trig)
- Chaos (Gate 6) t> Drum 6 (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Quantized melody with chaos injections
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 synced · quant = Major pentatonic root C · voltage window 2V (2 octaves) · gate into chaos in from a downbeat every 4 bars · melody re-rolls periodically for variation
patchIdeas.diagram:
Chaos:
* CH 1: synced 1:1
* Scale: Major pentatonic
* Root: C
* Voltage window: 2V

Clock:

Clock Divider:

- Clock (Out) c> Chaos (clock in)
- Clock Divider (/16) t> Chaos (chaos in) // re-roll every 4 bars
- Chaos (CV 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Chaos (Gate 1) g> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Frozen 8-step loop jam
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 running chaotically · press loop · hold loop + encoder to set length 8 · now a fixed melody loops · hold loop + width to alter width in-loop without breaking the pattern
patchIdeas.diagram:
Chaos:
* CH 1: loop ON
* Loop length: 8
* Scale: Minor pentatonic

Clock:

- Clock (Out) c> Chaos (clock in)
- Chaos (CV 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Chaos (Gate 1) g> Envelope (Trig)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-patched pseudo-sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: Gate 6 into chaos in · CH 1-5 assigned to chaos input · each time CH 6 fires, CH 1-5 re-roll · low entropy ~20% on modifiers gives controlled evolution (described in manual as 'pseudo-controlled' random)
patchIdeas.diagram:
Chaos:
* CH 1-5: chaos-in assigned
* CH 1-5: entropy 20%
* CH 6: prob 30%, time 2:1

- Clock Source (Out) c> Chaos (clock in)
- Chaos (Gate 6) t> Chaos (chaos in) // self-patch for evolution
- Chaos (CV 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Chaos (CV 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Turing machine from loop
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 synced · capture 16 steps via loop · quantized chromatic · voltage window 1V · behaves like a Turing Machine with 16-step lock, and loop modifiers can drift probability/width over time while pitch stays locked
patchIdeas.diagram:
Chaos:
* CH 1: synced 1:1, loop ON
* Loop length: 16
* Scale: Chromatic
* Voltage window: 1V

Clock:

- Clock (Out) c> Chaos (clock in)
- Chaos (CV 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Chaos (Gate 1) g> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: External LFO shaped through scale
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 input assign = CV-in Sample-and-Hold mode · LFO into CV in · quantize to Dorian · LFO shape becomes a rising/falling melodic line through the scale · no slew since source is external
patchIdeas.diagram:
Chaos:
* CH 1: CV-IN S&H mode
* Scale: Dorian
* Root: D

LFO:

Clock:

VCO:

Envelope:

- Clock (Out) c> Chaos (clock in)
- LFO (Out) >> Chaos (cv in) // shaped through scale
- Chaos (CV 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Chaos (Gate 1) g> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: CV-scanned slot morph
patchIdeas.patch: Save 10 variations in a bank · patch a slow LFO (0-8V) into CV in · CV-in routed to slot recall (global mode) · morph between 10 distinct patches under LFO control · produces evolving long-form structure
patchIdeas.diagram:
Chaos:
* CV in: slot recall mode
* Active bank: 1

Clock:

LFO:

- Clock (Out) c> Chaos (clock in)
- LFO (Out · 0-8V) >> Chaos (cv in) // scans 10 slots
- Chaos (CV 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Chaos (CV 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Chaos (Gate 1) g> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Controlled chaos modulation bank
patchIdeas.patch: All 6 channels unsynced · ground transpose staggered · voltage windows at 3V, 5V, 8V, etc · each CV out modulates a different destination (cutoff, resonance, pan, FM depth) for six independent organically-drifting modulations
patchIdeas.diagram:
Chaos:
* CH 1-6: unsynced
* CH 1: time 200ms, window 3V
* CH 2: time 1s, window 5V
* CH 3-6: various

Filter:

VCA:

VCO:

Delay:

Reverb:

- Chaos (CV 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Chaos (CV 2) >> Filter (Reso)
- Chaos (CV 3) >> VCA (CV)
- Chaos (CV 4) >> VCO (FM)
- Chaos (CV 5) >> Delay (Time)
- Chaos (CV 6) >> Reverb (Mix)
